title: Sorengia anceps
intro: Sorengia anceps, also known as Western yarrow, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Western yarrow has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Western yarrow include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Western yarrow is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being added to topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
taxonomy: Sorengia anceps is classified as a species within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Sorengia, which is a type of grass. More specifically, Sorengia anceps is a species of grass that is native to North America.
